preamble: The palatalized voiced alveolar lateral approximant [lʲ] can undergo a process of delateralization, evolving into the voiced palatal approximant [j]. This shift often occurs as the lateral articulation is lost, and the palatalization, which is already a prominent feature of [lʲ], becomes the sole defining characteristic of the resulting sound.
sources: Cresci, M. (2014). VN > V in Camuno: An alternative historical pathway to nasal loss. Italian Journal of Linguistics.
